title: Learning to Say No
subtitle: 学会说不：为什么拒绝比同意更有价值

paragraphs.en: Not doing something will always be faster than doing it. This philosophy applies in many areas of life. For example, there is no meeting that goes faster than not having a meeting at all. This is not to say you should never attend another meeting, but the truth is that we say yes to too many things we don't actually want to do.
paragraphs.zh: 不做某事永远比做它来得更快。这一哲理适用于生活的诸多领域。例如，再短的会议也没有压根不开会来得高效。这并非说你再也不该参加任何会议，但事实是，我们对太多本不想做的事情说了"是"。

paragraphs.en: How often do people ask you to do something and you just reply, "Sure." Three days later, you're overwhelmed by how much is on your to-do list. We become frustrated by our obligations even though we were the ones who said yes to them in the first place. Even worse, people will occasionally fight to do things that waste time. You don't have to do something just because it exists. It's worth asking if things are necessary. Many of them are not, and a simple no will be more productive than whatever work the most efficient person can cope with. But if the benefits of saying no are so obvious, then why do we say yes so often?
paragraphs.zh: 有多少次别人让你做某事，你脱口就是"没问题"？三天之后，你却被待办清单上的事情压得喘不过气来。我们对自己的义务感到沮丧，尽管当初说"是"的正是我们自己。更糟的是，人们偶尔还会抢着去做一些浪费时间的事。你不必仅仅因为某件事存在就去做它。值得一问的是：它真的必要吗？许多事情其实毫无必要，一个简单的"不"字比最高效的人所能应付的任何工作都更具生产力。但如果说"不"的好处如此明显，那我们为什么还频频说"是"呢？

words.meaning: adj. 不堪重负的；应接不暇的

paragraphs.en: We say yes to many requests not because we want to do them, but because we don't want to be seen as rude or unhelpful. Often, we have to consider saying no to someone we will interact with again in the future — our co-worker, our spouse, our family and friends. Saying no to our superiors at work can be particularly difficult. In these situations, I like the approach recommended in Essentialism by Greg McKeown. He writes, "Remind your superiors what you would be neglecting if you said yes and force them to deal with the trade-off. For example, if your manager comes to you and asks you to do X, you can respond with 'Yes, I'm happy to make this the priority. Which of these other projects should I deprioritize to pay attention to this new project?'" Collaborating with others is an important element of life. The thought of straining the relationship outweighs the commitment of our time and energy. For this reason, it can be helpful to be gracious in your response. Do whatever favors you can, and be warm-hearted and direct when you have to say no. But even after we have accounted for these social considerations, many of us still seem to do a poor job of managing the trade-off between yes and no. We find ourselves over-committed to things that don't meaningfully improve or support those around us, and certainly don't improve our own lives.
paragraphs.zh: 我们对许多请求说"是"，并非真的想做，而是不想显得粗鲁或不愿助人。通常，我们不得不考虑对那些日后还要打交道的人说"不"——同事、配偶、家人和朋友。对工作中的上级说"不"尤其困难。在这种情况下，我喜欢格雷格·麦基翁在《精要主义》一书中推荐的方法。他写道："提醒你的上级，如果你说'是'，你将不得不搁置哪些事项，迫使他们做出取舍。例如，如果经理来找你做某件事，你可以回答：'好的，我很乐意优先处理这件事。不过，我该把其他哪些项目的优先级往后放，才能腾出精力来完成这项新任务呢？'"与人合作是生活的重要组成部分。担心关系紧张往往压过了我们对时间精力投入的考量。因此，在回应时保持得体颇有助益。能帮的忙尽量帮，但不得不说"不"时，要既暖心又直接。然而，即便我们顾及了这些社交因素，许多人似乎仍然不擅长在"是"与"否"之间做出权衡。我们发现自己过度投入于那些既不能切实改善或支持身边人、也肯定不能提升自我生活的事情。

paragraphs.en: Perhaps one issue is how we think about the meaning of yes and no. The words yes and no get so often used in comparison with each other that it feels like they carry equal weight in conversation. In reality, they're not just opposite in meaning, but of entirely different magnitudes in commitment. When you say no, you're only saying no to one option. When you say yes, you're saying no to every other option. I like how economist Tim Harford put it, "Every time we say yes to a request, we're also saying no to anything else we might accomplish with the time." Once you're committed to something, you've already decided how that future block of time will be spent. In other words, saying no saves you time in the future. Saying yes costs you time in the future. No is a form of time credit. You retain the ability to spend your future time however you want. Yes is a form of time debt. You have to pay back your commitment at some point.
paragraphs.zh: 或许问题之一在于我们对"是"与"否"含义的理解。这两个词常被相提并论，让人感觉它们在对话中分量相当。但实际上，它们不仅意义相反，在承诺的程度上更有天壤之别。当你说"不"时，你只是对这一个选项说"不"。而当你说"是"时，你相当于对其他所有选项都说了"不"。我喜欢经济学家蒂姆·哈福德的说法："每次我们对一个请求说'是'，我们也就在对这段时间内可能完成的任何其他事情说'不'。"一旦你承诺了某事，你就已经决定了未来那段时间将如何度过。换句话说，说"不"为你节省了未来的时间。说"是"则耗费了你未来的时间。"不"是一种时间信用。你保留了按自己意愿支配未来时间的能力。"是"则是一种时间债务。你必须在未来某个时刻偿还你的承诺。

paragraphs.en: No is a decision. Yes is a responsibility. Saying no is sometimes seen as a luxury that only those in power can afford. And it's true: turning down opportunities is easier when you can fall back on the safety net provided by power, money, and authority. But it's also true that saying no is not merely a privilege reserved for the successful. It's also a strategy that can help you become successful. Saying no is an important skill to develop at any stage of your career because it retains the most important asset in life: your time. As investor Pedro Sorrentino put it, "If you don't guard your time, people will steal it from you." You need to say no to whatever isn't leading you toward your goals.
paragraphs.zh: "不"是一个决定。"是"是一种责任。说"不"有时被视为只有掌权者才能享有的奢侈。的确，当你有权力、金钱和地位构筑的安全网作后盾时，拒绝机会会更容易。但同样真实的是，说"不"并不仅是成功人士的特权。它也是一种可以帮助你走向成功的策略。在职业生涯的任何阶段，说"不"都是一项需要培养的重要技能，因为它能保留人生最重要的资产：你的时间。正如投资人佩德罗·索伦蒂诺所说："如果你不守护自己的时间，别人就会从你这里偷走它。"你需要对任何无法引领你迈向目标的事情说"不"。

paragraphs.en: Nobody embodied this idea better than Steve Jobs, who said, "People think focus means saying yes to the thing you've got to focus on. But that's not what it means at all. It means saying no to the hundred other good ideas that there are. You have to pick carefully." Jobs had another great quote about saying no: "I'm actually as proud of the things we haven't done as the things I have done. Innovation is saying no to 1,000 things."
paragraphs.zh: 没有人比史蒂夫·乔布斯更能体现这一理念。他曾说："人们以为专注就是要对必须专注的事情说'是'。但完全不是那么回事。专注意味着对其他一百个好主意说'不'。你必须仔细挑选。"乔布斯还有一句关于说"不"的精彩名言："事实上，我对自己没做过的事情和已经做过的事情一样感到自豪。创新就是对1000件事说'不'。"

paragraphs.en: Over time, as you continue to improve and succeed, your strategy needs to change. The opportunity cost of your time increases as you become more successful. At first, you just eliminate the obvious distractions and explore the rest. As your skills improve and you learn to separate what works from what doesn't, you have to continually increase your threshold for saying yes. You still need to say no to distractions, but you also need to learn to say no to opportunities that were previously good uses of time, so you can make space for better uses of time. It's a good problem to have, but it can be a tough skill to master.
paragraphs.zh: 随着时间的推移，当你不断进步、取得成功时，你的策略也需要改变。随着你越来越成功，时间的机会成本也随之增加。起初，你只需剔除明显的干扰项，其余的尽管探索。但随着能力提升，学会分辨有效与无效之后，你就必须不断提高说"是"的门槛。你仍需对干扰说"不"，但同时也得学会对那些曾经算得上善用时间的机会说"不"，从而为更高效的时间利用腾出空间。能遇到这种问题是好事，但掌握这项技能却并非易事。

paragraphs.en: What is true about health is also true about productivity: an ounce of prevention is worth a pound of cure. More effort is wasted doing things that don't matter than is wasted doing things inefficiently. And if that is the case, elimination is a more useful skill than optimization. I'm reminded of the famous Peter Drucker quote, "There is nothing so useless as doing efficiently that which should not be done at all."
paragraphs.zh: 健康领域的真理同样适用于生产力：一分预防胜过十分治疗。浪费在无关紧要之事上的精力，远多于效率低下造成的浪费。既然如此，学会舍弃就比学会优化更有价值。我想起了彼得·德鲁克的那句名言："没有什么比高效地去做根本不该做的事更无用了。"
